title: phlox multiflora subsp. patula
intro: Phlox multiflora subsp. patula, also known as creeping phlox, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to the southeastern United States, characterized by its clusters of pink, purple, or white flowers that bloom in early spring. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain and swelling, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of creeping phlox include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies and teas. Creeping phlox can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
